title: Database Languages Classification and Flow
description: A flowchart showing the classification of database languages and their sequential role in the database lifecycle.
relationships: DDL defines the structure first (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
DML then manipulates the data within the structure (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
DQL is a subset of DML used specifically for querying (SELECT)
DCL manages access and permissions (GRANT, REVOKE)
TCL controls the transactions, ensuring changes are saved or undone (COMMIT, ROLLBACK)
